stories.title: Colorado Springs 4th of July 2026 Family Guide — Star-Spangled Symphony, Rock Ledge Ranch & More

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: July 4 in COS: Star-Spangled Symphony at Ford Amphitheater (gates 5:30 PM, fireworks ~9 PM) or free at Rock Ledge Ranch (10 AM–4 PM, Civil War encampment, kids parade). America's 250th. [URL] #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Free July 4 option in COS: Rock Ledge Ranch, 10 AM–4 PM. Kids patriotic parade, Civil War encampment, live music, historic home tours. No tickets needed. [URL] #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

**Key Facts (Verified):**
- [HIGH] Star-Spangled Symphony at Ford Amphitheater, 95 Spectrum Loop, COS 80921
- [HIGH] Date: Saturday July 4, 2026; Gates open 5:30 PM; show starts 6:30 PM; fireworks ~9:00 PM
- [HIGH] Performers: Fort Carson's 4th Infantry Division Band; Colorado Springs Philharmonic
- [HIGH] Program includes: Dolly Parton, Marvin Gaye, Tina Turner hits; Star Wars suite; 1812 Overture; Stars and Stripes Forever
- [HIGH] Presented by Colorado Springs Philharmonic and Colorado Springs Sports Corporation
- [HIGH] Rock Ledge Ranch Historic Site: July 4, 10 AM–4 PM; Civil War encampment; kids patriotic parade; historic home tours; games
- [HIGH] Palmer Lake Centennial Park: July 4, 4–10 PM; free; food, vendors, giveaways, family activities
- [HIGH] 2026 is America's 250th birthday (Semiquincentennial)
- [MEDIUM] Ticket prices not specifically confirmed in search results; available at fordamphitheater.live
**Freshness:** July 4 is 11 days away. High-engagement parenting content for COS families.

stories.title: RECALL ALERT — ABC Trading Toy Recall: Light-Up Glasses and Electronic Pet Toys Pose Battery Ingestion Hazard

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: ABC Trading light-up glasses and pet cage toys (~85,000 units). Button cell batteries — accessible to kids, deadly if swallowed. Stop use. Photo in trash. Email recallabc@gmail.com for refund. cpsc.gov [URL] #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: CPSC recall: ABC Trading light-up glasses + pet cage toys. Models 8266 + ZH998-23. $5–$9 at TOYZ stores, Nov 2022–Oct 2025. Button cell battery ingestion = internal chemical burns. Call 323-581-3688. [URL] #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Recalls ABC Trading Light-Up Glasses and Electronic Pet Toys — Button Cell Battery Hazard

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
ABC Trading Toy Recall

#### Image Concept 2A — Social (1080x1350, X)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** Close-up flat-lay of a small light-up toy and colorful child's toy on a clean white background, bright studio lighting, red warning banner implied through composition — clean space in the lower third for text overlay, photorealistic product-safety visual style, no text on image, slightly cool clinical light.

#### Image Concept 2B — Social (1200x630, Facebook)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A concerned parent hand holding a small colorful electronic toy over a bin, abstract minimalist style, neutral soft-gray background, clean product safety visual, bright even lighting, space in the lower portion for text overlay, no text, no faces visible, calming but serious mood.

---

research.facts:
CPSC Recall — ABC Trading Toy Battery Hazard
**Sources:**
- cpsc.gov — "ABC Trading Recalls WSDZ Light-up Glasses Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion" (official CPSC page)
- cpsc.gov — "ABC Trading Recalls Children's Toys Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion" (official CPSC page)
- foodpoisoningbulletin.com — "ABC Trading Children's Toys Recalled For Battery Ingestion Hazard"
- newsweek.com — "Children's Toys Recalled Over Death Risk"
**Key Facts (Verified):**
- [HIGH] Products recalled: WSDZ Light-up Glasses; Electronic Pet Cage — Dinosaur Tribes (model 8266, silver/blue cage, red dinosaur, yellow egg); My Pet Bird Cute Bird Tribes (model ZH998-23, pink cage, blue bird)
- [HIGH] Approximately 84,700 units affected
- [HIGH] Hazard: battery compartments easily accessed by children; button cell/coin batteries cause internal chemical burns and death when swallowed
- [HIGH] Sold at TOYZ and Joissu Product stores nationwide; November 2022–October 2025; $5–$9
- [HIGH] Action: stop use immediately; photo of disposed product in trash; email recallabc@gmail.com for full refund
- [HIGH] Contact: 323-581-3688 (Monday–Friday 9:30 AM–6:00 PM PT)
- [HIGH] Website: abctradinginc.com/recall-2
- [HIGH] CPSC at cpsc.gov/Recalls
**Freshness:** Active recall. Different product category from June 22 GOPO recall. Critical safety information.

stories.title: Hot Car Safety — Temperatures Rise, Children at Risk; What COS Parents Need to Know

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: 9 children died in hot cars in 2026 already. A car heats 20°F in 20 minutes. Kids' bodies heat 3–5x faster than adults. Always check the back seat. Always. [URL] #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Hot Car Safety 2026: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know This Summer

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Hot Car Safety

#### Image Concept 4A — Social (1080x1350, X)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** Interior view of an empty car back seat on a bright hot summer day, harsh sunlight streaming through windows, a small empty child's car seat, warm orange-yellow light suggesting dangerous heat, slightly unsettling but not graphic, flat-lay photographic style, clean space in the lower third for text overlay, no text, no child visible.

#### Image Concept 4B — Social (1200x630, Facebook)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** Parent's hand reaching back toward empty rear car seat in a bright, sun-filled parking lot setting, warm direct summer sunlight, safety reminder visual metaphor, photorealistic style, clean lower portion for text overlay, no text on image, gentle urgency without being alarming.

---

research.facts:
Hot Car Safety Summer 2026
**Sources:**
- kidsandcars.org — "2026 Child Hot Car Deaths" (tracking database)
- nhtsa.gov — "Child Heatstroke Prevention: Prevent Hot Car Deaths" (federal guidance)
- wdbj7.com — "Safety experts urge parents to stay alert as hot car deaths rise" (June 3, 2026)
- abc7.com — "Rising summer heat increases risk of child deaths in hot cars" (safety tips)
**Key Facts (Verified):**
- [HIGH] 9 children died from vehicular heatstroke in 2026 (as of reporting; deaths occurred in CA, FL, LA, MD, NM, NJ, NC per abc7.com)
- [HIGH] Children's bodies heat 3–5 times faster than adults
- [HIGH] Cars can heat 20°F in 20 minutes
- [HIGH] Children face serious risk when internal body temperature reaches 107°F
- [HIGH] Majority of cases: caregiver accidentally left child (loss of awareness, not neglect)
- [HIGH] NHTSA prevention tips: always check back seat before locking; place phone or bag next to car seat; never leave children alone even briefly
- [MEDIUM] Colorado Springs late June temperatures reach 80s–90s°F (source: weather.gov climate averages; not independently confirmed for 2026 specifically)
**Freshness:** June-August is peak risk period; COS summer temperatures underway; 2026 death toll is current.

research.facts:
D49 Student Success Center
**Sources:**
- d49.org — "D49 Student Success Center Opens August of 2026" (official D49 announcement post)
**Key Facts (Verified):**
- [HIGH] D49 will open Student Success Center (SSC) in August 2026 for the 2026-2027 school year
- [HIGH] Location: former Falcon Elementary School of Technology building
- [HIGH] Full-time preschool: two classrooms, Monday–Thursday; Base49 provides before/after-school care + Friday childcare
- [HIGH] Elevates program: serves students ages 18–21 in transition services (essential life skills, community experiences)
- [HIGH] Also housed: Motor Team (physical therapists, occupational therapists, assistants); Assistive Technology Team; SWAP Coordinator; homebound special education teacher
- [MEDIUM] D49 website confirms announcement but detailed capacity/enrollment info not confirmed independently
**Freshness:** D49 school year planning; opens August 2026; relevant for families with preschool-age or special needs students.
